title: Agentic Support Dispatch
lede: Turn user feedback and UX paper cuts into active pull requests without waiting on the engineering backlog.

highlights.label: Avg. Turnaround
highlights.detail: From ticket dispatch to drafted PR

sections.title: Eligible Tickets
sections.description: Incoming support tickets automatically flagged as low-complexity UX fixes suitable for agent dispatch.
rows: Update button colour on pricing page to match brand guidelines
Typo in the password reset email template
Inconsistent scroll behaviour on the mobile navigation menu
sections.emptyMessage: No eligible tickets currently in the queue.

sections.title: Dispatched Agents
sections.description: Track the progress of AI agents acting as junior engineers on assigned tickets.

sections.title: Recent Activity Log
sections.description: Audit trail of agent orchestration and completed fixes.
items: Agent Alpha merged PR #99: Fixed padding alignment on settings modal.
Agent Beta assigned to Ticket #4190 by Support Lead.
Agent Alpha submitted PR #102 for engineering review.
System flagged 3 new tickets as agent-eligible.

demoSteps: Review the 'Eligible Tickets' queue to see auto-flagged paper cuts.
Simulate clicking 'Dispatch Agent' on a high-confidence ticket (e.g., #4192).
Observe the ticket move from the queue into the 'Dispatched Agents' table with a 'Drafting Code' status.
View the completed PR output link, illustrating how non-technical staff hand off the final review to senior engineers.
notes: Ensure the demo clearly communicates that code is drafted but never auto-deployed without human engineering review.
Use UK spelling conventions (e.g., 'colour', 'behaviour') throughout the hardcoded fixtures.
The UI should look like an internal support tool, not a complex IDE.
